data.title: Bitcoin Trail and Uber Eats Deliveries Help Tie Florida Man to Steam Malware Case
data.description:
Federal investigators say a Bitcoin trail, Google cookies, and more than 500 Uber Eats deliveries helped identify Zyaire Dontaevious Zamarion Wilkins as the alleged financier and marketer of a Steam malware campaign. According to NS3.AI, federal agents arrested Wilkins, 21, in Florida on July 14.

A search also uncovered a Monero seed phrase linked to roughly $382,000 in cumulative transaction activity.

data.title: STOCKS | Waymo Considers Ending Partnership With Uber, FT Says
data.description: According to Jin10, the Financial Times reported that Waymo is considering ending its partnership with Uber as tensions in the robotaxi sector deepen.

data.title: Waymo Considers Ending Uber Partnership as Competition Intensifies
data.description:
According to Jin10, British Financial Times reported that Alphabet-owned self-driving company Waymo is exploring options to exit its partnership with Uber.

According to Jin10, multiple people familiar with the matter said the company has internally discussed ending the agreement under which Uber operates services in Austin and Atlanta, and Uber said Waymo has notified it that it plans to enter those two markets independently from January 2028, if the contract allows.

data.title: Devon Mulls $4 Billion Exit From Eagle Ford, Powder River
data.description:
Devon Energy is exploring a sale of its Eagle Ford and Powder River shale assets as it streamlines its portfolio, with the two fields potentially fetching more than $4 billion, according to Bloomberg.

People familiar with the matter said the assets are part of Devon's broader effort to simplify its holdings.

data.title: STOCKS | Philadelphia Semiconductor Index Falls as Much as 5%
data.description: The Philadelphia Semiconductor Index fell as much as 5%. According to Jin10, it briefly widened its losses to that level.

data.title: Google Raises Concerns Over Recent European Commission Decisions
data.description: Google said it is concerned about the impact of recent European Commission decisions and thanked the U.S. government for its involvement. According to Jin10, the company made the remarks in response to the issue.

data.title: Google Seeks to Comply With EU Digital Markets Act Requirements
data.description: According to Jin10, Google is working to comply with the requirements of the EU Digital Markets Act (DMA).

data.title: STOCKS | S&P 500 Wavers as Chip Rout Offsets Oil Decline
data.description:
US stocks finished a jittery week little changed as a selloff in chipmakers offset a decline in oil and signs of strength in Corporate America’s profit engine, according to Bloomberg.

The move left the S&P 500 wavering as traders weighed pressure on semiconductor shares against softer energy prices.

data.title: Brazilian Planning Minister Moretti: Government Will Reassess Its Oil Export Tax Stance if Brent-Related Indicators Ease Again
data.description: According to Jin10, Brazilian Planning Minister Moretti said the government will reassess its stance on the oil export tax once Brent-related indicators ease again.

data.title: Iranian Media Reports Several Explosions in Northern Kuwait
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian media outlet Fars News reported that several explosions occurred in northern Kuwait.

data.title: U.S. Media: Intelligence Agencies Assess Iran's New Leadership Has Greater Nuclear Ambition
data.description: According to Jin10, the New York Times reported, citing officials familiar with the assessment, that U.S. intelligence agencies believe Iran's new Supreme Leader Mojtaba is more eager than his father, former Supreme Leader Khamenei, to develop nuclear weapons, and that Khamenei had vowed to abandon nuclear weapons development during his lifetime. U.S. intelligence agencies also believe Mojtaba and the hard-line government that took power afterward have ambitions to develop advanced nuclear weapons after the U.S. and Israel jointly killed several former Iranian leaders. If the U.S.-Israel war makes Tehran more likely, rather than less likely, to seek nuclear weapons, that would be another serious consequence of the assassination of Khamenei and the Trump administration's initial regime-change push.

data.title: Iran Accuses U.S. Military of Mistakenly Striking Cargo Ship, Killing Two Crew Members
data.description: According to Jin10, Iranian Mehr News Agency reported that on Friday afternoon local time, the U.S. military fired two missiles at an LPG carrier entering the area from the Gulf of Oman. The ship was reportedly mistaken for carrying Iranian gas, and the attack killed two crew members, damaged the vessel's engine room, and forced it to stop sailing.

data.title: Brazilian Planning Minister Moretti Says Higher Brent Prices Should Lift Tax Revenue
data.description: According to Jin10, Brazilian Planning Minister Moretti said higher Brent crude prices should boost tax revenue, but the outlook remains uncertain.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Claude Opus 5 Launches at $5 Per Million Input Tokens, Tops Major Benchmarks
data.description: Claude Opus 5 launched at $5 per million input tokens and outperformed Fable 5 on most major benchmarks. According to NS3.AI, Opus 5 scored 43.3% on Frontier-Bench v0.1, compared with 33.7% for Fable 5 and 34.4% for OpenAI's GPT-5.6 Sol.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Shanghai Gold and Silver Futures Close Higher; SC Crude Oil Falls
data.description: At the 2:30 close, the main Shanghai gold futures contract rose 0.18% to 885 yuan per gram, and the main Shanghai silver futures contract gained 1.39% to 14,323 yuan per kilogram. According to Jin10, the SC crude oil main contract fell 2.55% to 578 yuan per barrel.

data.title: Iranian Official Denies Rumors of Another U.S. Attack on Shalamcheh Border Crossing
data.description: According to Jin10, an assistant to the deputy governor of Iran's Khuzestan province said rumors that the U.S. had again attacked the Shalamcheh border crossing on the Iran-Iraq border were untrue.

data.title: Salesforce jumps 4% after $1.6 billion Veterans Affairs contract
data.description: According to CNBC, Salesforce shares rose more than 4% after the company said it won a three-year, $1.6 billion contract from the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. The agreement gives VA employees agentic AI-based tools aimed at improving efficiency. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria said the deal appears significant because of its size and the department's importance, but he warned that one contract does not change Salesforce's growth challenges and does not necessarily ensure meaningful profits. Salesforce has said its public sector cloud annual recurring revenue topped $2 billion for the first time in May, up 23% year over year. The stock remains down nearly 40% in 2026 despite rising more than 6% over the past month.

data.title: STOCKS | Stocks Rise With Bonds as Oil Falls Below $100
data.description: Wall Street staged a late-week comeback, with stocks and bonds both higher as oil fell below $100, according to Bloomberg. Corporate America’s profit machine continued to show signs of strength, helping calm a jittery market.

data.title: Ex-VW Engineers Charged Over Rivian Venture Insider Trading
data.description:
Two former Volkswagen AG engineers were arrested over insider-trading bets placed before the German automaker announced a $5 billion joint venture with Rivian Automotive Inc., according to Bloomberg.

The case centers on trades made ahead of Volkswagen’s disclosure of the planned partnership with the electric-vehicle maker.

data.title: Google Ordered to Defend Robby Starbuck AI Defamation Suit
data.description:
Alphabet Inc.'s Google LLC must defend a lawsuit filed by Robby Starbuck over alleged false statements made by an artificial intelligence chatbot, according to Bloomberg.

The ruling Friday denied Google's bid to escape the case, which could expose the company to legal liability for chatbot-generated defamation.
